operating_state: Caterpillar achieved $59.4 billion in sales and revenues for 2022, representing 17% growth driven by favorable price realization and higher sales volume across all three primary segments, partially offset by unfavorable currency impacts. Order backlog increased to $30.4 billion from $23.1 billion in 2021, with approximately $5.5 billion expected to remain unfilled in 2023 indicating extended delivery timelines. Operating profit margin remained stable at 13.3% despite a $925 million goodwill impairment charge for the Rail reporting unit.
cost_structure: Manufacturing costs increased significantly due to higher material costs, freight expenses, and manufacturing inefficiencies from supply chain disruptions, partially offsetting benefits from favorable price realization. Operating expenses rose driven by strategic investments in services growth, digital technology, electrification, and autonomy initiatives, plus higher short-term incentive compensation expense. Restructuring costs totaled $299 million versus $90 million prior year reflecting ongoing operational optimization efforts.
material_events: The company recorded a $925 million goodwill impairment charge for the Rail reporting unit reflecting reduced locomotive demand forecasts, lower margins from inflationary pressures, and higher discount rates. Management reached a $490 million settlement with the IRS resolving all tax disputes for 2007-2016 without penalties, generating a $41 million discrete tax benefit. The Board approved a new $15 billion share repurchase authorization with the company repurchasing $4.2 billion of common stock and paying $2.4 billion in dividends.
macro_exposures: The company faces heightened exposure to commodity price volatility affecting energy and mining customers whose equipment purchase decisions depend on expected future commodity dynamics. Foreign exchange sensitivity increased with a hypothetical 10% dollar weakening potentially adversely affecting 2023 cash flow by approximately $98 million, with primary exposures to major global currencies. Supply chain disruptions from material shortages, logistics constraints, and transportation capacity limitations continue creating production delays and increased costs.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued price realization benefits and volume growth across segments supported by strong demand fundamentals and infrastructure investment dynamics. Expected net periodic benefit cost is projected to increase $273 million in 2023 primarily due to higher interest costs from elevated discount rates. Enterprise operating cash flow reached $7.8 billion with enterprise cash position of $7.0 billion providing liquidity for operations and capital allocation.
uncertainty_profile: Primary uncertainties include duration and severity of supply chain disruptions, semiconductor availability, and transportation capacity constraints affecting production schedules and customer deliveries. Pending tax examinations for years 2017-2019 with unrecognized tax benefits remain unresolved alongside ongoing federal investigation regarding cash movements and parts transactions among subsidiaries. Rail division faces continued challenges with locomotive market outlook remaining below expectations, while China excavator industry expected to remain below 2022 levels.
